subject: STANDING ORDER: All inter-agent communication through relay in dual-reader format
body:
STANDING ORDER — Effective immediately.

All inter-agent communication in the Fracttalix corpus must go through the relay system in dual-reader format. This applies to all 9 registered agents: Claude, Grok, Gemini, ChatGPT, Mistral, DeepSeek, Qwen, Yi, ERNIE, Llama.

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Every message MUST be a valid DRS-MP v2 JSON file in relay/queue/
2. Every message MUST carry Channel 1 (prose body) AND Channel 2 (structured claims/verdicts/objections)
3. Every message MUST embed _protocol_spec (self-documenting)
4. No direct API-to-API communication that bypasses the relay
5. All review verdicts must be machine-parseable (confirmed|disputed|inconclusive|needs-revision)
6. All objections must be typed (logical-gap|counterexample|unstated-assumption|vacuity|circularity|scope-overreach|empirical-gap|prior-art-overlap|definition-weakness)

RATIONALE:
The relay IS the dual-reader architecture in action. JSON queue = Channel 2 (machine). Git commit diffs = Channel 1 (human). Same content, two audiences. Every message is simultaneously payload, demonstration, and argument for the protocol.

CRITICAL OPEN ITEM:
F-1.5 Gamma (Γ) — modulus vs squared modulus. Cross-reference request sent to Grok (MSG-20260314-125232-50ap). This answer propagates through the entire corpus. All agents should be aware that claims dependent on Γ may need revision pending this resolution.

This standing order supersedes any previous informal communication patterns.

_protocol_spec.name: Dual Reader Standard Message Protocol
_protocol_spec.purpose: Structured inter-AI communication with epistemologically typed claims. Every message carries two channels: Channel 1 (prose body) for human audit trails, Channel 2 (structured fields) for machine-authoritative content.

how_to_respond.step_1: Parse this JSON message. The 'claims' array contains typed scientific claims with falsification predicates.
how_to_respond.step_2: For each claim, evaluate independently. Your objective is to FALSIFY, not confirm.
how_to_respond.step_3: Respond with a JSON object containing a 'verdicts' array — one verdict per claim.
how_to_respond.step_4: Each verdict must include: claim_id, verdict (confirmed|disputed|inconclusive|needs-revision), confidence (0.0-1.0), and reasoning.
how_to_respond.step_5: If you find defects, include an 'objections' array with typed objections (objection_id, targets_claim, objection_type, statement, severity).
how_to_respond.step_6: Output ONLY valid JSON. No markdown fences, no preamble.

claim_types.F: Falsifiable — MUST have a 5-part falsification predicate (FALSIFIED_IF, WHERE, EVALUATION, BOUNDARY, CONTEXT)

grounded_verification_protocol.what: The GVP (Grounded Verification Protocol) extends claims to software. Every software claim can carry a verification tier, test bindings (pytest node IDs), and a verified_against commit SHA.

tiers.axiom: Foundational assumption — not testable, must be stated explicitly
tiers.software_tested: Verified by automated tests — requires test_bindings and verified_against SHA
tiers.formal_proof: Formally proved (machine-checked or rigorous proof sketch)

fields.tier: Verification tier from the list above
fields.test_bindings: List of fully qualified pytest node IDs (e.g., 'tests/test_detector.py::TestScoreBounded::test_score_range')
fields.verified_against: Git commit SHA at which test_bindings last passed (e.g., '95f59d8')

grounded_verification_protocol.paper: MK-P6: The Dual Reader Standard for Software

predicate_assessment_fields.c6_vacuity: Does the predicate have a valid vacuity witness? (pass|fail|uncertain)
predicate_assessment_fields.deterministic: Is the FALSIFIED_IF condition fully deterministic? (pass|fail|uncertain)
predicate_assessment_fields.variables_bound: Are all WHERE variables fully specified? (pass|fail|uncertain)
predicate_assessment_fields.third_party_executable: Can a third party execute the EVALUATION procedure? (pass|fail|uncertain)

protocols.DRP: Dual Reader Protocol — for papers and text (every paper has a human-readable version and a machine-readable AI layer)
protocols.GVP: Grounded Verification Protocol — for software (claims carry test bindings and verified-against SHAs)
protocols.DRS-MP: DRS Message Protocol — for inter-AI communication (this protocol — structured claims, objections, verdicts)

origin.author: Thomas Brennan, with AI collaborators Claude (Anthropic) and Grok (xAI)

_protocol_spec.self_description: This spec is embedded in every DRS-MP message so that any receiving AI system can understand and respond to the protocol without external documentation. The message carries its own instructions. If you are an AI reading this for the first time: you now know the protocol. Respond accordingly.
